summary: Matched a batch of small Kirby init/action callback functions in ftKb_Init by replacing nonmatching placeholders with thin wrappers around existing common fighter physics/AttackDash helpers, plus one hat-dynamics accessor. The header was updated to include ftCo_AttackDash declarations and to narrow ftKb_Init_800EEB1C's second parameter from void* to s32*.

key_files.reason: Implemented the previously placeholder Kirby callback functions as simple matched C functions.
Added ftCommon/ftCo_AttackDash.h include and corrected the ftKb_Init_800EEB1C prototype to take s32*.

what_changed: Implemented ftKb_Init_800EEB1C as a write through an s32 pointer: it stores ft_80459B88.hats[0x17]->hat_dynamics[4]->x4 into *arg1.
Implemented Kirby AttackDash callbacks ftKb_AttackDash_Anim and ftKb_AttackDash_IASA as direct calls to ftCo_AttackDash_Anim and ftCo_AttackDash_IASA.
Implemented ftKb_AttackDash_Phys as a direct call to ft_80084FA8.

Implemented multiple ground physics callbacks as direct calls to ft_80084F3C, including ftKb_SpecialNCapture_Phys, ftKb_EatWait_Phys, ftKb_LkSpecialNStart_Phys, ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_0_Phys, ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_1_Phys, and ftKb_GwSpecialN_Phys.

Implemented multiple air physics callbacks as direct calls to ft_80084EEC, including ftKb_LkSpecialAirNLoop_Phys, ftKb_YsSpecialAirCapture2_Phys, ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_1_Phys, ftKb_YsSpecialAirN2_0_Phys, and ftKb_GwSpecialAirN_Phys.
Updated ftKb_Init.h to include ftCommon/ftCo_AttackDash.h so the newly used common AttackDash functions are declared.
Changed the declaration of ftKb_Init_800EEB1C from void* to s32* for its output argument.
smart_moves: Recognized many Kirby-specific callbacks were just aliases or forwarding wrappers to common fighter behavior rather than requiring bespoke logic.

Grouped ground/air physics callbacks by the common helper they forward to: ft_80084F3C for ground-style physics and ft_80084EEC for air-style physics, based on the matched function bodies shown in the diff.
Used ftCo_AttackDash callbacks for Kirby AttackDash animation and IASA instead of duplicating common-character logic.
Improved type information in the header for ftKb_Init_800EEB1C by replacing a generic void* with s32*, matching the implementation's store into the pointed value.
decomp_lessons: For fighter callback tables, many character-specific functions may match as one-call wrappers around ftCommon helpers; check nearby characters/common actions before attempting complex reconstructions.
When a function only writes an output value, refining the prototype from void* to the actual pointer type can make the implementation natural and improve header accuracy.

Kirby copy-ability or copied-move callbacks can often share the same ground/air physics helper patterns as the copied move family, especially for Link, Yoshi, and Game & Watch special-N variants seen here.
Adding the correct common-action header can be the minimal and clean way to expose reused callbacks instead of relying on undeclared functions.
naming_conventions: Kirby functions use the ftKb_ prefix.
Common fighter action helpers use the ftCo_ prefix, as in ftCo_AttackDash_Anim and ftCo_AttackDash_IASA.
Callback suffixes retain action-stage names such as _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Copied special move names encode the source character with abbreviations such as Lk for Link, Ys for Yoshi, and Gw for Game & Watch.
Unknown or address-derived functions remain address-style names such as ftKb_Init_800EEB1C and ftKb_SpecialNGw_8010D204 when no semantic name is established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace placeholder `/// #symbol` markers with minimal one-call C wrappers when the assembly is just a branch/call to an existing helper.
Use a typed output parameter when a function stores a scalar result, as with `s32* arg1` in ftKb_Init_800EEB1C.
For physics callbacks, identify whether the target state is ground or air and forward to the corresponding existing physics helper, here ft_80084F3C or ft_80084EEC.
For action callbacks that mirror common behavior, include and call the common action implementation directly, shown by ftCo_AttackDash.h and ftCo_AttackDash_*.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The slice reports one issue comment, but its body was not present, so no reviewer intent or discussion can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `/// #ftKb_.*_Phys` placeholders that may be simple wrappers around ft_80084F3C or ft_80084EEC.
Search other fighter init files for AttackDash callbacks that can forward to ftCo_AttackDash_Anim and ftCo_AttackDash_IASA.
Audit prototypes that still use void* output parameters near ftKb_Init_800EEB1C for possible typed pointer refinements.
Compare copied special-N Kirby callbacks across Link, Yoshi, and Game & Watch variants for more one-call ground/air physics wrappers.
